children.text: 18.00 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for the 2008 crop year;
18.25 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for the 2009 crop year;
18.50 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for the 2010 crop year;
18.75 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for each of the 2011 through 2018 crop years;
19.75 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for each of the 2019 through 2024 crop years; and
24.00 cents per pound for raw cane sugar for each of the 2025 through 2031 crop years.
22.9 cents per pound for refined beet sugar for the 2008 crop year;
a rate that is equal to 128.5 percent of the loan rate per pound of raw cane sugar for the applicable crop year under subsection (a) for each of the 2009 through 2024 crop years; and
a rate that is equal to 136.55 percent of the loan rate per pound of raw cane sugar under subsection (a)(6) for each of the 2025 through 2031 crop years.

children.label: A
children.text: the end of the 9-month period beginning on the first day of the first month after the month in which the loan is made; or

children.label: B
children.text: the end of the fiscal year in which the loan is made.

children.label: A
children.text: be made at the loan rate in effect at the time the first loan was made; and

children.label: B
children.text: mature in 9 months less the quantity of time that the first loan was in effect.

children.heading: Nonrecourse loans
children.label: 1 Nonrecourse loans
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out this section through the use of nonrecourse loans.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall obtain from each processor that receives a loan under this section such assurances as the Secretary considers adequate to ensure that the processor will provide payments to producers that are proportional to the value of the loan received by the processor for the sugar beets and sugarcane delivered by producers to the processor.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the Secretary may establish appropriate minimum payments for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: In the case of sugar beets, the minimum payment established under clause (i) shall not exceed the rate of payment provided for under the applicable contract between a sugar beet producer and a sugar beet processor.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: The Secretary may not impose or enforce any prenotification requirement, or similar administrative requirement not otherwise in effect on , that has the effect of preventing a processor from electing to forfeit the loan collateral (of an acceptable grade and quality) on the maturity of the loan.May 13, 2002

children.heading: Definition of in-process sugars and syrups
children.label: 1 Definition of in-process sugars and syrups
children.text: In this subsection, the term “in-process sugars and syrups” does not include raw sugar, liquid sugar, invert sugar, invert syrup, or other finished product that is otherwise eligible for a loan under subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make nonrecourse loans available to processors of a crop of domestically grown sugarcane and sugar beets for in-process sugars and syrups derived from the crop.

children.heading: Loan rate
children.label: 3 Loan rate
children.text: The loan rate shall be equal to 80 percent of the loan rate applicable to raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar, as determined by the Secretary on the basis of the source material for the in-process sugars and syrups.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As a condition of the forfeiture of in-process sugars and syrups serving as collateral for a loan under paragraph (2), the processor shall, within such reasonable time period as the Secretary may prescribe and at no cost to the Commodity Credit Corporation, convert the in-process sugars and syrups into raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar of acceptable grade and quality for sugars eligible for loans under subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: Transfer to corporation
children.label: B Transfer to corporation
children.text: Once the in-process sugars and syrups are fully processed into raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar, the processor shall transfer the sugar to the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: the loan rate for raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar, as appropriate; and

children.label: II
children.text: the loan rate the processor received under paragraph (3); by

children.label: ii
children.text: the quantity of sugar transferred to the Secretary.

children.heading: Loan conversion
children.label: 5 Loan conversion
children.text: If the processor does not forfeit the collateral as described in paragraph (4), but instead further processes the in-process sugars and syrups into raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar and repays the loan on the in-process sugars and syrups, the processor may obtain a loan under subsection (a) or (b) for the raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar, as appropriate.

children.heading: Term of loan
children.label: 6 Term of loan
children.text: The term of a loan made under this subsection for a quantity of in-process sugars and syrups, when combined with the term of a loan made with respect to the raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar derived from the in-process sugars and syrups, may not exceed 9 months, consistent with subsection (c).

children.heading: Avoiding forfeitures; Corporation inventory disposition
children.label: f Avoiding forfeitures; Corporation inventory disposition

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to subsection (d)(3), to the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall operate the program established under this section at no cost to the Federal Government by avoiding the forfeiture of sugar to the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To carry out paragraph (1), the Commodity Credit Corporation may accept bids to obtain raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar in the inventory of the Commodity Credit Corporation from (or otherwise make available such commodities, on appropriate terms and conditions, to) processors of sugarcane and processors of sugar beets (acting in conjunction with the producers of the sugarcane or sugar beets processed by the processors) in return for the reduction of production of raw cane sugar or refined beet sugar, as appropriate.

children.heading: Bioenergy feedstock
children.label: B Bioenergy feedstock
children.text: If a reduction in the quantity of production accepted under subparagraph (A) involves sugar beets or sugarcane that has already been planted, the sugar beets or sugarcane so planted may not be used for any commercial purpose other than as a bioenergy feedstock.

children.heading: Additional authority
children.label: C Additional authority
children.text: The authority provided under this paragraph is in addition to any authority of the Commodity Credit Corporation under any other law.

children.heading: Duty of processors and refiners to report
children.label: 1 Duty of processors and refiners to report
children.text: A sugarcane processor, cane sugar refiner, and sugar beet processor shall furnish the Secretary, on a monthly basis, such information as the Secretary may require to administer sugar programs, including the quantity of purchases of sugarcane, sugar beets, and sugar, and production, importation, distribution, and stock levels of sugar.

children.heading: Proportionate share States
children.label: A Proportionate share States
children.text: As a condition of a loan made to a processor for the benefit of a producer, the Secretary shall require each producer of sugarcane located in a State (other than the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico) in which there are in excess of 250 producers of sugarcane to report, in the manner prescribed by the Secretary, the sugarcane yields and acres planted to sugarcane of the producer.

children.heading: Other States
children.label: B Other States
children.text: The Secretary may require each producer of sugarcane or sugar beets not covered by subparagraph (A) to report, in a manner prescribed by the Secretary, the yields of, and acres planted to, sugarcane or sugar beets, respectively, of the producer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall require an importer of sugars, syrups, or molasses to be used for human consumption or to be used for the extraction of sugar for human consumption to report, in the manner prescribed by the Secretary, the quantities of the products imported by the importer and the sugar content or equivalent of the products.

children.heading: Tariff-rate quotas
children.label: B Tariff-rate quotas
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to sugars, syrups, or molasses that are within the quantities of tariff-rate quotas that are subject to the lower rate of duties.

children.label: i
children.text: information on the production, consumption, stocks, and trade of sugar in Mexico, including United States exports of sugar to Mexico; and

children.label: ii
children.text: publicly available information on Mexican production, consumption, and trade of high fructose corn syrups.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: The data collected under subparagraph (A) shall be published in each edition of the World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates.

children.heading: Penalty
children.label: 5 Penalty
children.text: Any person willfully failing or refusing to furnish the information required to be reported by paragraph (1), (2), or (3), or furnishing willfully false information, shall be subject to a civil penalty of not more than $10,000 for each such violation.

children.heading: Monthly reports
children.label: 6 Monthly reports
children.text: Taking into consideration the information received under this subsection, the Secretary shall publish on a monthly basis composite data on production, imports, distribution, and stock levels of sugar.

children.heading: Substitution of refined sugar
children.label: h Substitution of refined sugar
children.text: For purposes of Additional U.S. Note 6 to chapter 17 of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States and the reexport programs and polyhydric alcohol program administered by the Secretary, all refined sugars (whether derived from sugar beets or sugarcane) produced by cane sugar refineries and beet sugar processors shall be fully substitutable for the export of sugar and sugar-containing products under those programs.

children.heading: Effective period
children.label: i Effective period
children.text: This section shall be effective only for the 2008 through 2031 crops of sugar beets and sugarcane.
